sources.exact_document_title: Evaluation and Treatment of Hirsutism in Premenopausal Women: An Endocrine Society Clinical Practice Guideline
sources.version: Journal of Clinical Endocrinology & Metabolism 2018;103(4):1233–1257; DOI 10.1210/jc.2018-00241
sources.jurisdiction: International specialty guideline; UAE laboratory, prescribing and referral policy governs local use
sources.population: Premenopausal women with patient-important hirsutism or an abnormal hirsutism score.
sources.clinical_setting: Clinician evaluation and longitudinal review of hirsutism in endocrine, primary-care or reproductive-health services.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for documenting progression, menstrual or hyperandrogenic context, patient-important impact, clinician-selected androgen testing and clinician-discussed review. It does not establish a cause or authorise treatment.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1–1.3 — diagnosis and biochemical evaluation
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting an abnormal hirsutism score, progression, menstrual disturbance, other hyperandrogenic features, family or ethnic context and clinician-selected androgen or 17-hydroxyprogesterone testing.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 2.1–2.8 and 3.1–3.9 — patient-important impact, treatment discussion and reassessment
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports patient-important symptom impact, preferences, treatment-response review and clinician-led reassessment. Medicine, contraception, cosmetic procedures and treatment instructions are excluded from mappings.

archive.population: Premenopausal women with patient-important hirsutism or an abnormal hirsutism score.
archive.setting: Clinician evaluation and longitudinal review of hirsutism in endocrine, primary-care or reproductive-health services.
